ks1.objective: Throw your ball at the wall and race your partner to touch the cone with it after it bounces.
successCriteria: Tracks own ball after the bounce

lks2.objective: Develop throwing control and reaction speed — make the bounce return towards you, then beat your partner to the cone.
successCriteria: Throws bounce back towards the cone

space.description: Pair stands a short distance from a wall, cone between them. Pairs spaced out along the wall so throws don't collide.

steps: Pair up. Each pair has one tall cone (the 'Christmas tree') and one tennis ball each (the 'stars').
Stand a short distance from a wall, with the cone placed between both players.
On a 3–2–1 countdown, both children throw their tennis balls against the wall.
After the bounce, players race to be the first to touch the top of the cone with their ball.
First player to touch the cone earns 1 point. Reset and repeat.
Play to a set number of points or for a set time.
questions: Can you control your throw so the ball bounces back towards you?
Can you explain the quickest way to get your ball to the top of the cone?

step.space: Move closer or further from the wall — change the rebound time
step.task: All players must be seated to play (chair-based PE)
step.equipment: Use beanbags or cones for throwing instead of balls
step.people: Assign the role of scorer to one child (rotate roles)
